key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 consolidated net income $872.7M ÷ 51.087M shares = $17.08/sh; Adjusted EBITDA $1536.2M consolidated
Connected Living mobile growth and lender-placed housing persist; 12× exit on year-10 owner cash

open_questions: Confirm whether FY2025 catastrophe-light Housing earnings are normalized or peak-cycle.
Pending subsidiary sale loss magnitude and timing (10-K restructuring disclosure).
No approved third-party sources; re-scan when Substacks or fund letters added.
thesis: Assurant is a specialty insurance and extended-protection company that distributes through partners rather than selling directly to consumers. **[Fact]** The company operates two reportable segments: **Global Lifestyle** (Connected Living mobile device solutions, extended service contracts, Global Automotive vehicle protection) and **Global Housing** (lender-placed homeowners, manufactured housing, flood, and renters insurance). **[Fact]** FY2025 consolidated revenues were **$12.8 billion** and net income **$872.7 million** on **51.1 million** diluted shares. **[Fact]** The business model is B2B2C: Assurant embeds protection products inside carrier, lender, retailer, and OEM channels across North America, Latin America, Europe, and Asia Pacific. **[Inference]** Economics come from recurring premiums and fees on large installed bases, plus investment income on marked fixed-income portfolios backing unearned premium reserves.
why_market_wrong: The stock trades near **16 times** trailing earnings while the market may underweight Assurant's **Connected Living mobile attach** and **lender-placed housing franchise** durability. **[Fact]** Global Housing Adjusted EBITDA rose **28%** in FY2025 to **$858.7 million**, helped by lower reportable catastrophes and lender-placed policy growth; Global Lifestyle Adjusted EBITDA grew **4%** to **$801.3 million** on Connected Living mobile programs. **[Inference]** Conversely, the market may overweight near-term catastrophe-light housing results while underpricing mobile partner concentration and a pending subsidiary divestiture loss noted in the 10-K. **[Fact]** Debt to total capital is **27.3%** with net debt only **~$373 million** after **$1.8 billion** cash, limiting financial distress risk. **[Inference]** No dated HK predictive attribute exists; the edge is **specialty franchise quality at a modest premium**, not a catalyst with a known payoff date.
